global.title: Events & Spaces
global.description: Our events are where it all happens. We meet to learn, create, consult, and sometimes just to have a drink and feel at home. Whether you're looking for an intense tech challenge or a chill evening of bar-side talks, we have a place for you.

categories.title: DYKEATHON
categories.description: 'We are here to make sure we have each other's backs.' Our mission is simple: to create a network that actually works for us. We don't wait for an invite to the table; we're building our own. This means:
features: Being the first point of contact: Whether you need a job referral, help prepping for an interview, or just want to talk through a professional dilemma with someone who truly gets it.
Opening the door: Helping anyone who wants to break into tech realize it's possible, and showing them the best way to get there.
No explanations needed: Creating events and meetups where our identity is the default, not something we have to justify or explain.

categories.title: MEETUPS
categories.description: Regular gatherings that keep our community connected. Panel talks, tech demos, community updates, and space to just show up as yourself — no explanations needed.
features: Panel talks: Hear from queer women leading tech, building companies, and making change.
Community updates: Stay in the loop on what's happening across the LGBTQ+ tech space.
Low-pressure networking: Meet people, find your people, and leave with a warmer heart.

categories.title: DRINK & DYKE
categories.description: Our casual, no-agenda bar nights. These events are all about unwinding, making friends, and enjoying a relaxed evening together.

events.title: Dykeathon 2026
about: The Dykeathon is a 24-hour hackathon for queer women and non-binary people in tech. We come together to build, create, and make lasting connections — because we don't wait for an invite to the table; we build our own.

This year we're partnering with the LGBTQ+ Center in Tel Aviv to bring the community together for a day of innovation, challenge, and solidarity. Whether you're a developer, designer, product manager, or just curious about tech — there's a place for you here.
All skill levels are welcome. All we ask is that you show up ready to connect and create.

events.title: Dykeathon 4.0
about: 14 LGBTQ+ organizations, each matched with a dedicated team of queer women in tech to build real tools for their day-to-day work.

Organizations spanned community, health, mental health, sports, legal, and advocacy — from grassroots groups to established centers. Teams brought fullstack engineers, frontend developers, data specialists, designers, and automation experts.
From WhatsApp bots to scraping tools, Monday integrations to custom web platforms — every project shipped with a real organization behind it.

events.title: Psycho-Tech relations
about: 200+ participants were thrilled to hear our 3 amazing women share about psychological and medical therapy alongside high-tech.
A workshop was held on a coding software that integrates a language model — hands-on, practical, and energizing.
One of our biggest meetups yet. The community keeps growing.

program.title: Doors Open
program.description: Food, drinks, and mingling.

program.title: Speakers — Therapy Meets Tech
program.description: 3 speakers sharing about psychological and medical therapy alongside high-tech innovation.

program.title: Workshop — AI-Integrated Coding
program.description: Hands-on workshop on a coding software that integrates a language model.

program.title: Open Networking
program.description: Connect, collaborate, and keep the conversation going.

events.title: Dykeathon 3.0
events.tagline: Tech for those who fight for us
about: Bringing together queer women in product and tech to build real solutions for 10 LGBTQ+ and community organizations.

Teams worked directly with organizations including Beit El-Mim, Achva, the TLV LGBTQ+ Center, Rakafot, and community groups responding to the events of October 7th — building tools that ranged from digital archives and volunteer management platforms to WhatsApp bots and housing portals.
Projects like the Gay Media Archive, SeniorVIP, and the Shared Parenting Portal showed just how much is possible when queer women in tech show up for their community.

events.title: Women Innovation at War
about: Hosted at the Pagaya office, 150 participants were thrilled to hear our four speakers talking about women innovation at war times.
The evening also served as a teaser for Dykeathon 3.0, coming in April 2024 — with a first look at the challenge tracks and organizations we'd be working with.
Beer, pizza, and sufganiyot provided. Spots were limited, community was full.

program.title: Doors Open
program.description: Food, drinks, and catching up with the community.

program.title: Panel — Women Innovation at War Times
program.description: Hear from 4 speakers talking about women innovation at war times — projects that made a difference.

program.title: Dykeathon 3.0 Teaser
program.description: A first look at what's coming in April 2024 — the organizations, the challenges, the vision.

program.title: Open Networking
program.description: Mingle, connect, and eat sufganiyot. Happy Hanukkah.

events.title: Dykeathon 2.0
about: Over 200 participants on board to solve the biggest problems of LGBTQ+ non-profit organizations — creating production-ready tools for organizations to implement in their day-to-day activities.

8 organizations participated: Bet El-Mem (Arab LGBTQ+), Maavarim (trans advocacy), Haguda, the TLV LGBTQ+ Center, Hoshen, LGBTech, Achva, and the Gila Project for Trans Empowerment. Each org came with a C-level leader and a real challenge.
This edition leaned heavily into GenAI and LLMs, with teams building faster and smarter than ever before. Bet El-Mem took first place, Maavarim second, and Haguda third.

program.title: Day 1 — Kickoff & Team Formation
program.description: Introductions, challenge briefings, and matching PMs with organizations.

program.title: Hacking Begins
program.description: 24-hour build sprint starts. Teams work directly with org representatives.

program.title: Lunch
program.description: Food, mentors, and energy drinks to keep everyone going.

program.title: Late-Night Checkpoint
program.description: Mentors circulate, teams share progress and blockers.

program.title: Day 2 — Final Push
program.description: Morning of the final day — wrapping up builds, preparing presentations.

program.title: Project Presentations
program.description: Each team presents to judges and the full community.

program.title: Awards & Closing
program.description: Winners announced. Community celebration.

events.title: Dykeathon 1.0
about: The very first Dykeathon — 100 participants got to know 4 major issues of the LGBTQ community: Safety, Legal, Community IRL, and Health, and created MVPs for products addressing those problems.
Teams were guided by domain experts: Dr. Ruth Gofen (Health), Adv. Michal Eden (Legal), Ayala Azulay (Safety), and Noa Dagan (Community).

Standout MVPs included LesBMe in Health (🥇), BigSister in Community (🥈), Standart in Safety (🥉), and HelpMeSue in Legal. The night ended with an after-party at Shpagat — and the knowledge that we'd do it all again, bigger.

program.title: Doors Open & Registration
program.description: Coffee, food, and meeting your teammates.

program.title: Opening Ceremony
program.description: Welcome, challenge briefings, and team formation across 4 domains.

program.title: Hacking Begins
program.description: Teams get to work with domain experts available for consultation.

program.title: Lunch Break
program.description: Shared lunch. Domain experts host office hours.

program.title: Afternoon Sprint
program.description: Final build push. Mentors in the room.

program.title: Project Presentations
program.description: Each team presents to judges and the community.

program.title: Awards Ceremony
program.description: Winners announced in Health, Safety, Community, and Legal categories.

program.title: After Party — Shpagat
program.description: The celebration continues until 02:00.

organizations.name: בת קול
organizations.about: ארגון לנשים דתיות גאות, המעניק מרחב בטוח לשיחות על אמונה, מגדר ונטייה מינית, ומאחד נשים מהקשת הלסבית, ביסקסואלית וטרנסית שומרות מסורת.

organizations.name: פרוייקט גילה
organizations.about: הארגון הטרנסי הראשון והוותיק ביותר לקידום זכויות והעצמת הקהילה הטרנסית בישראל, שהוקם בשנת 2010 מתוך הקהילה ולמענה

organizations.name: האחווה הסטודנטיאלית הגאה - Achva - LGBTI Student's Association in Israel
organizations.about: האחווה הסטודנטיאלית הגאה היא התארגנות סטודנטיאלית להט\"בית הפועלת ביותר מ-30 קמפוסים ברחבי הארץ, יוצרת קהילות ומרחבי שייכות, ופועלת למען שינוי חברתי עבור הקהילה הגאה.

organizations.name: Hoshen
organizations.about: חוש\"ן — חינוך ושינוי הוא ארגון החינוך וההסברה של הקהילה הגאה, המוביל מדי שנה מעל 3,000 סדנאות ב-450 בתי ספר, ומגיע ל-100,000 תלמידים ואנשי מקצוע ברחבי ישראל.

organizations.name: ישראל 2050
organizations.about: ישראל 2050 פועלת לשיפור המציאות הכלכלית-חברתית בישראל על ידי חיזוק ההשפעה האזרחית על המערכת הדמוקרטית, ויוצרת רשת אזרחית שתשנה את מאזן הכוחות לטובת האינטרס הציבורי הרחב.

organizations.name: LGBTech Ltd.
organizations.about: LGBT WORK הוא ארגון ללא מטרות רווח מוביל בישראל, המתמחה בקידום גיוון והכללה של קהילת הלהט\"ב בשוק העבודה, ופועל ליצירת סביבות עבודה מכילות, שוויוניות ומגוונות.

organizations.name: תהלה
organizations.about: עמותת תהל"ה תומכת בהורים ובבני משפחה של לסביות, הומוסקסואלים, א.נשים על הקשת הטרנסית וביסקסואלים, דרך קו הקשב הטלפוני וקבוצות תמיכה.

organizations.name: עמותת אותות
organizations.about: בית חם למעל 1,500 בני נוער במצבי סיכון ומשבר מידי שנה, עם דלת פתוחה וטיפול רגיש תרבות לכלל החברה הישראלית.

organizations.name: LGBTQ TLV center
organizations.about: המרכז הגאה בתל אביב מציע מגוון רחב של שירותים לקהילה הלהט\"ב: תרבות ואירועים, חוגים וקורסים, מרכז למשפחה, קבוצות חברתיות ומענה קהילתי הכולל טיפול, עזרה סוציאלית ומיצוי זכויות.

organizations.name: תוצרת הארץ
organizations.about: תוצרת הארץ היא תנועת קהילות הצעירים של ישראל, המאמינה שחיים קהילתיים הם הדרך לשיקום ולחיזוק החברה הישראלית, ומקימה קהילות משימתיות ברחבי הארץ כבר למעלה מ-13 שנים.

organizations.name: Beit El-Meem
organizations.about: Beit El-Meem is an open home for all sexual and gender identities, supporting and fighting against the oppression of the Arab LGBTQ+ community, offering a support line, legal services, and psychological and social support.

organizations.name: האגודה למען הלהט"ב בישראל
organizations.about: האגודה למען הלהט"ב בישראל היא ארגון ארצי חלוצי המוביל את מאבקה הציבורי של הקהילה הגאה לזכויות, מלווה קהילות גאות מקומיות ברחבי הארץ, ומספק מענה לכל להט"ב בכל צורך שהוא.

organizations.name: מעברים
organizations.about: ארגון מעברים לקשת הטרנסית הוא מרכז הידע הטרנסי הגדול בישראל, הפועל להעצמת הקשת הטרנסית באמצעות ידע ומשאבים, ומנגיש מידע קריטי לאנשים על הקשת הטרנסית, לבני משפחתם ולאנשי מקצוע.

organizations.name: עמותת זיכרון 710
organizations.about: זיכרון 710 הוקם כדי לאסוף ולשמר עדויות אירועי ה-7 באוקטובר בזמן אמת — הודעות ווטסאפ, תמונות וסרטונים — ולבנות ארכיון חי המספר את הסיפור האישי והקהילתי של אותו יום.

projects.description: Effortless Allyship Against Transphobia Transphobic hate and misinformation are increasing online. 76% of trans respondents to a 2023 survey experienced online harassment in their lifetimes - the highest of any demographic surveyed.
